label: plain_text
text:
In fact, any equation of the form p(x) = O, where p(x) i
is a polynomial of degree
2, is a quadratic equation. But when we write the terms of p(x) in descending order of
their degrees, then we
get the standard form of the equation. That is, ax² + bx + c =
0
a ≠ O is called the standard f
form
of a quadratic e
equation.
